changes.before: Deep wagon ruts shoved the horses onto the firmer verge... Cyndia's mare carried the same crust around the bit.
changes.after: Jaina rode low over the gelding's neck... The horse's mouth had gone white around the bit.
changes.reason: Compressed duplicated footing and tack detail so the ticking-clock pressure lands faster.

changes.before: If we can take the northern service road into the warehouse district, we start there. Biggest stockpiles first. Anything still stacked.
changes.after: Northern service road into the warehouse district. Biggest stock first. Anything still in sacks.
changes.reason: Cut planning-note phrasing and duplicated logistics nouns from Jaina's spoken line.

changes.before: Then we go where the bread leaves heat.
changes.after: Then we go where the bread's still hot.
changes.reason: Made the line sound spoken instead of translated from analysis.

changes.before: Any yard with district carts still loading. If I can spoil a full stock before it moves, that quarter loses the next wave.
changes.after: Any yard with carts still loading. If children are already eating in the doorways, that block is gone.
changes.reason: Replaced logistics-summary speech with human-scale field language.

changes.before: Long barns, mill sheds, kitchen plots... Jaina had run the map a hundred times in her head.
changes.after: Then the wind shifted. Warm yeast. Woodsmoke. Sweetness under both.
changes.reason: Dropped explanatory setup and let smell carry the realization.

changes.before: Beauty had nothing to do with it... The place was only a building again.
changes.after: The inward pull at the center of the store dragged once against the staff, then snapped.
changes.reason: Cut editorial commentary and kept only the physical change Jaina experiences.

changes.before: District rounds... two church kitchens, and private contracts along Festival Lane.
changes.after: District runs... two church kitchens, houses on Festival Lane.
changes.reason: Pulled the clerk out of memo language into spoken merchant diction.

changes.before: After that we count. No more guessing off the map.
changes.reason: Removed plan-speak and tightened the pivot.

changes.before: the command it had just heard
changes.reason: The whistle setup already established the control signal.

changes.before: Rolls. Still warm... More loaves, baskets, and hands crossed in front of her faster than she could hold them in her head.
changes.after: All around them, warm bread kept moving... Another boy crossed the intersection with baskets hooked over each arm.
changes.reason: Kept the realization but cut the second inventory pass.

changes.before: Jaina kept her attention on the lane ahead because the words were easier thrown at stone than at Sylvanas's face.
changes.after: Jaina kept her eyes on the lane ahead because the words were easier thrown at stone than at Sylvanas's face.
changes.reason: Shifted from abstract attention to a concrete physical anchor.

changes.before: The method works on a single store.
changes.after: I can ruin a store if I reach it in time.
changes.reason: Replaced report diction with urgent first-person capability.
Removed commercial-summary vocabulary from Jaina's spoken explanation.

observations.note: Opening approach spent too long on ruts, verges, and tack before the chapter reached the actual clock; compressed so the pressure lands by paragraph three.
Arthas's distance was already doing the work; the extra sentence about Jaina checking the dust smear repeated the urgency.
The first route briefing carried the same plan twice: technical version, then simplified version. Polish pass kept only the usable version.
The 'How late?' / 'Smaller words.' exchange slowed the launch without adding new tactical information.
The pre-gate city panorama repeated smoke, kitchens, and appetite before the wind-shift image landed; folded into one sensory turn.
Changed 'broken down into' to 'gone to' for a harder verb and shorter opening sentence.
Removed duplicated stock nouns: 'stockpiles,' 'stacked,' and later 'full stock' were stacking the same idea.
Kept one three-beat sensory paragraph ('Warm yeast. Woodsmoke. Sweetness under both.') because it grounds the chapter's threat cleanly.
The first warehouse description had enough place-detail without 'rose into beams and shadow' plus loft language; one image was enough.
Cut 'Beauty had nothing to do with it' because the sentence explained Jaina's urgency after the surrounding prose had already made it obvious.
The spoil sequence originally split one magical action across two explanatory waves; tightened it so the break lands once.
Dropped 'the place was only a building again' because it summarized what the snapped pull already told the reader.
The bread-in-the-street realization originally arrived, then arrived again with bread steam, then again with the second warehouse inventory. Reduced to one clean escalation.
Changed 'landed harder' to 'hit harder' to keep the narration in a plainer, more physical register.
Changed 'warehouse man at a glance' to 'laborer at a glance' so the sentence sounds like Jaina seeing a body, not classifying an employee.
Removed 'the command it had just heard' because the whistle setup already established causality; the gloss was vestigial.
Changed 'contamination' to 'taint' in narration. Jaina can think precisely without sounding like a report.
Cut 'There was no other way left' because the entire scene already proved that point.
Changed 'surviving stock' to 'What remained' to avoid administrative diction after the emotional turn had already happened.
Compressed the southern-gate tableau; the chapter did not need the crest, gatework, and oath image all at full length before Arthas spoke.
Jaina's early dialogue in this chapter wanted to drift toward logistics-briefing language; Sylvanas's 'Say it in streets' line gave the rewrite its ceiling.
The clerk's speech could hold workman vocabulary, but 'private contracts' sounded memo-written; 'houses on Festival Lane' kept the same meaning in a lived register.
Jaina's answer to Arthas now speaks in first-person limits ('I can ruin a store') instead of method-summary language.
Kept 'street packet' in the clerk exchange; it reads like local trade jargon rather than institutional technocracy.
Kept 'district' in dialogue where it names actual city subdivisions, not abstract logistics.
No spoken line now uses zero-tolerance offenders such as actionable, vector, optimal, propagation, or ingested.
Sylvanas stayed clipped without collapsing into one-word bark mode; her brevity still feels chosen.
Jaina's final refusal to Arthas simplified cleanly; no extra argument, no policy language, just the answer.

flags: Line 65: 'felt' retained — callback to Hearthglen bodily memory, not a present-moment filter stack.
Participial attachments stayed at 3, which remains inside the chapter cap; left in place where simultaneity mattered.
One 'like' construction remains at the gate ('looked less like another officer') because it sharpens Jaina's judgment rather than fogging action.
